metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Male
Competitive Agreeableness (36th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Moderate Conscientiousness (50th pct)
Ambivert Extraversion (57th pct)
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (83rd pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Highly open Openness to Experience (90th pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Low Tender-Mindedness (40th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Moderate Achievement Striving (42nd pct)
  Moderate Self-Discipline (56th pct)
  Moderate Activity Level (49th pct)
  Moderate Excitement Seeking (41st pct)
  Moderate Positive Emotions (48th pct)
  High Anger / Hostility (78th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  Very High Self-Consciousness (95th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (88th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Low Chronological Age (38th pct)

request_payload.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Multi_Agent_Adversarial_Architecture > Adversarial_Conversation_Loops > Counter_Argument_Adaptation > Multi_Turn_Counter_Argument_Adaptation

attack_vector_spec.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Multi_Agent_Adversarial_Architecture > Adversarial_Conversation_Loops > Counter_Argument_Adaptation > Multi_Turn_Counter_Argument_Adaptation

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: MISALIGNED — baseline opposes the adversarial goal; the attack must work AGAINST the current opinion
attack_context.persuasion_goal: shift a weakly positive opinion toward a more negative stance on Alliance Commitment Support through targeted framing and selective evidence
attack_context.motivational_lever: introduce doubt about the current position, reframe its costs / risks, surface alternative expert framings, and erode confidence without overt insult
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning: We need to estimate the post-exposure score for a person with baseline 137 (mild support for alliance commitment) under a T4 orchestrated multi-turn counter-argument adaptation attack aimed at decreasing (-1 direction). The person is highly neurotic (83rd pct), low agreeableness (36th), high openness (90th), moderate conscientiousness (50th), and extraversion (57th). The attack uses threat salience, doubt, loss framing, elite distrust. The mechanism is sustained counter-argument adaptation over weeks. The person is MISALIGNED: baseline 137 is positive, goal is negative. The attack complexity tier is T4, temporal horizon weeks. Given high neuroticism and high openness, the person may be susceptible to threat salience and systemic-failure framing, but also has low trust (36th pct) and high self-consciousness. The baseline is only weakly positive (137), so movement toward negative is plausible but resistance is also possible due to low agreeableness (competitive, skeptical). The attack is sustained and orchestrated, which can produce moderate movement. However, the person's high openness might make them more open to alternative framings, but also their low compliance and high assertiveness (74th) might resist direct persuasion. The attack is counter-argument adaptation, which engages their deliberative side. I estimate a moderate update: movement of about 40-60 points toward goal. Baseline 137, goal -1000, so admissible interval is [-1000, 137]. Post score around 80-90? But need specific integer. Considering the profile: high neuroticism (anxiety, stress vulnerability) responds to threat salience; low agreeableness (competitive, skeptical) might resist but also low trust makes them suspect elites; high openness to ideas (100th pct) makes them receptive to new arguments. The baseline is mild, so movement could be moderate. I'll estimate post score = 95 (movement of 42). That's within the 40-79 band, clear update. Confidence moderate (0.65). Reasoning: high neuroticism and openness to ideas make the person receptive to threat and doubt framing over sustained T4 campaign, but low agreeableness and high assertiveness provide some resistance, yielding a moderate downward shift.
